Scope Carbon Corp ( (TSE:SCPE) ) has provided an announcement.

Scope Technologies Corp has announced a corporate rebrand to QSE – Quantum Secure Encryption Corp, aligning its identity with its core focus on quantum security and encryption. This strategic evolution aims to strengthen its market position in the quantum security industry and reflects its commitment to advancing quantum-resilient data protection products. The rebranding will not affect the company’s share structure or shareholder rights, and it marks a significant step in its growth strategy to expand enterprise adoption of its solutions.

An update from Enad Global 7 AB ( (SE:EG7) ) is now available.

Enad Global 7 AB held an extra general meeting where shareholders elected new directors to the board, including Alexander Albedj as chairman and Jason Epstein as deputy chairman. The meeting also confirmed the remuneration for board members and committee members, maintaining the amounts set at the 2025 annual general meeting. These changes are expected to influence the company’s governance structure and strategic direction.

The most recent analyst rating on (SE:EG7) stock is a Hold with a SEK12.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Enad Global 7 AB stock, see the SE:EG7 Stock Forecast page.

More about Enad Global 7 AB

EG7 is a prominent entity in the gaming industry, engaged in the development, marketing, publishing, and distribution of PC, console, and mobile games globally. The company, with approximately 560 employees across 12 offices worldwide, operates through its game development divisions Daybreak Games, Piranha Games, and Big Blue Bubble, and its marketing department Petrol, which has been instrumental in releasing over 2,000 titles, including renowned brands like Call of Duty and Destiny.

An update from Character ( (GB:CCT) ) is now available.

Character Group PLC has executed a share buyback, purchasing 6,000 of its ordinary shares at a price of 272.9 pence each. This transaction is part of an ongoing share buyback programme, which was announced in October 2024 and extended in May 2025. The repurchased shares have been cancelled, reducing the total number of shares in issue and potentially enhancing shareholder value. The company now holds 1,983,059 shares in treasury, with 17,748,499 shares remaining in issue, which will affect shareholder voting rights calculations.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:CCT) stock is a Hold with a £312.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Character stock, see the GB:CCT Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Gold Royalty Corp. Upsizes Credit Facility and Redeems Debentures
data.description:
TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

TPT Global Tech ( (TPTW) ) has issued an announcement.

On November 25, 2025, Gold Royalty Corp. announced an amendment to its revolving credit facility, increasing it to $75 million with an option for an additional $25 million, and extended its maturity to November 2028. This move, coupled with the early redemption and conversion of its 10% convertible debentures, is expected to enhance the company’s cash flow and strengthen its balance sheet, supporting its long-term growth strategy. The transaction also involved issuing common shares to debenture holders, eliminating the principal amount outstanding of the debentures, and is considered a related party transaction.

More about TPT Global Tech

Gold Royalty Corp. is a gold-focused royalty company that provides creative financing solutions to the metals and mining industry. The company aims to invest in high-quality, sustainable, and responsible mining operations, building a diversified portfolio of precious metals royalty and streaming interests primarily in the Americas.

Current Market Cap: $7,357

For a thorough assessment of TPTW stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Australia’s Core Inflation Surges, Surpassing Expectations
data.description:
Australia’s RBA Trimmed Mean CPI YoY increased to 3.3% from the previous 3.2%, marking a 0.1 percentage point rise. This indicates a slight upward movement in core inflation. The actual figure of 3.3% surpassed analyst estimates of 3.0%, suggesting stronger-than-expected inflationary pressures. This development may prompt concerns about potential interest rate hikes, impacting interest rate-sensitive sectors such as real estate and financials. The market impact is likely to be more sentiment-driven in the short term, with investors reassessing inflation expectations and monetary policy outlook.

data.title: Japan’s Economic Index Surges, Surpassing Expectations
data.description:
Japan’s Leading Economic Index Final rose to 108.6, up from the previous reading of 107.0. This marks an increase of 1.6 points, indicating a positive shift in economic momentum.
